data.title: Tokyo Gas: To Focus on Company's Assets in East Texas, North Louisiana
StanChart's Results Show Consistency — Market Talk
data.description: Standard Chartered's investors should welcome the consistency of the bank's delivery on its earnings and capital returns, Jefferies says in a research note. The lender posted fourth-quarter pre-provision earnings that beat market views, with revenue growth in its wealth solutions and financial markets divisions standing out. It also announced a $1.5 billion buyback program, surpassing expectations. The unchanged guidance for 5% to 7% revenue growth in 2025 looks conservative given the fourth-quarter's net interest income growth rate and the strong start of the year for non-interest-income items, analysts write. Shares in London open 3.7% higher at 1,182 pence. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Hang Seng Index Closes 4.0% Higher at 23477.92
L'Air Liquide rises to 11-month high on 'decent' results, outlook
data.description:
** Shares in French industrial gases supplier L'Air Liquide rises as much as 3.5% to its highest since mid-March 2024, after its annual results and lifted guidance

** J.P. Morgan analysts deem the results to be overall "decent" pointing to an upside guidance on 2025 and 2026 consensus

** "It is uncharacteristic for Air Liquide to provide a quantitative margin improvement target at the start of the year. So, that they have chosen to do this now... shows perhaps a higher level of management confidence on the delivery of these targets", says JPM

** The analysts also highlight "good mid-term growth story" citing high business backlog and investment plans

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 10.1%​ YTD

data.title: India's Business Activity Hits Six Month High in February: HSBC Flash PMI
data.description:
Driven by the expansion in the services activity in February, India's private sector output rose at the fastest pace in six months since August 2024, according to the HSBC Flash India Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) data released on Friday.

Flash services PMI is an early estimate of the PMI for a country.

The HSBC Flash India Composite Output Index, a seasonally adjusted index that measures the month-on-month change in the combined output of India's manufacturing and service sectors, expanded to 60.6 in February against a final reading of 57.7 in January.

The survey, compiled by S&P Global, showed that job creation reached a record high in February.

The services sector's index rose to 61.1 in February, the highest since March 2024, compared with 56.5 recorded in January. On the other hand, flash manufacturing PMI slipped to 57.1 in the reported month from 57.7.

"The latest HSBC 'flash' PMI data also indicated stronger growth of aggregate sales, which exerted upward pressure on operating capacities and prompted companies to step up hiring," the report said.

It added that price indices moved in opposite directions, with a slowdown in cost inflation against a faster upturn in prices charged for goods and services.

data.title: Xiamen Xiangyu Completes Private Share Issuance
data.description:
Xiamen Xiangyu completed its private share issuance following the approval of summary and related documents by the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

The logistics services provider will proceed with the registration and custody of the newly issued shares as per regulations, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: Czechia Business Sentiment at Near 2-Year High
data.description:
The business confidence indicator in the Czech Republic increased by 0.5 percentage points to 98 in February 2025 from 97.5 in the previous month.

This marked the highest reading since April 2023, as sentiment strengthened in construction sector (114.7 vs 111.7%).

In addition, sentiment improved for manufacturers (93.5 vs 93.2) and retailers (99.9 vs 96.2).

Meanwhile, sentiment remained unchanged for service providers (100.5).

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Closes 6.5% Higher at 5859.30
France Business Climate Rises in February
data.description:
France’s business climate indicator rose to 96 in February 2025, up from 95 in the previous month, though it remained below its long-term average of 100.

Sentiment improved among manufacturers (97 vs. 96 in January) and service providers (98 vs. 96), while retailers' confidence remained unchanged (97), and morale weakened in the construction sector (98 vs. 99).

Meanwhile, the employment climate indicator fell to 94, down from 98 in January, marking its lowest level since March 2015, excluding the COVID period.

This decline was primarily driven by a drop in sentiment regarding future workforce size in services, excluding temporary work agencies.

data.title: Stellantis, Volkswagen, Tesla, BYD Face Italian Probe into EV Performance Claims
data.description:
By Helena Smolak

Italy's competition watchdog launched separate investigations into whether Stellantis Europe, Volkswagen Italia, BYD Industria Italia and Tesla Italy failed to inform customers about limitations of their electric-vehicle battery performances and warranties.

The Italian Competition Authority, or AGCM, said Friday that it was looking into whether the carmakers failed to fully disclose information on the loss of battery capacity of its electric vehicles or on the limitations of the conventional battery warranty. It will also assess whether the companies provided contradictory information on the mileage range of its electric vehicles.

AGCM officials and the special Antitrust Unit of Italy's law enforcement agency Guardia di Finanza conducted an inspection at the Italian headquarters of the four parties yesterday, the antitrust body said.

Stellantis, Volkswagen, Tesla and BYD Industria Italia didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Write to Helena Smolak at helena.smolak@wsj.com

data.title: Elekta Enters Deal for Radiation Therapy Tech with Moffitt Cancer Center
data.description:
Elekta (EKTA-B.ST) said Friday it secured an order from the US-based Moffitt Cancer Center for its magnetic resonance-guided linear accelerator.

Under the agreement, the Swedish radiation therapy company will deploy its Elekta Unity system and related software and service for the Tampa, Florida-based national cancer institute's magnetic resonance-guided adaptive radiation therapy program.

data.title: Sika rises after FY results defy tough markets
data.description:
** Shares in Sika are up 2.8% after the Swiss specialty chemicals maker reported solid FY results despite challenging end markets in construction and automotive

** Vontobel highlights a disproportionate increase in profitability, as Sika's full-year net profit of CHF 1.25 BLN is 3% above consensus expectations

** Sika's growth is primarily driven by acquisition effects, Baader Helvea says

** J.P. Morgan says investors may be disappointed by the FY 2025 outlook of 3% to 6% local currency sales growth, which deviates from the company's planned 6% to 9% local currency sales growth

** Vontobel sees the company's 2025 outlook as optimistic thanks to lower interest rates and product innovations

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 9.3% YTD

data.title: City of London Investment Trust Posts Lower Fiscal H1 Net Return
data.description:
City of London Investment Trust said Friday its net return and gross revenue for the first half of fiscal 2025 fell year over year.

Net return after taxation for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 55.9 million pounds sterling, compared with 133.0 million pounds a year ago. Return per share moved to 0.1132 pound from 0.2647 pound.

The British investment trust's gross revenue and capital gains were 62.8 million pounds, compared with 139.1 million pounds earlier.

Meanwhile, the board declared a second interim dividend of 0.0525 pound per share, payable on Feb. 28 to shareholders of record on Jan. 24. It paid 0.0505 pound per share a year ago.

data.title: Weak Eurozone PMI Data Might Have Little Effect on Bonds — Market Talk
data.description: Weak provisional purchasing managers' data for France, Germany and the eurozone shouldn't have too much effect on bonds, ING analysts say in a note. Weak readings could upset the recent better sentiment about the eurozone's economic outlook. Still, it would take a lot for markets to start thinking that interest rates could fall much more than currently expected. For some months now, markets have expected that the ECB will cut rates to between 1.75% and 2%. "Breaking that range would need more than one bad PMI reading," they say. The 10-year Bund yield falls 1 basis point to 2.524%, according to Tradeweb. France's PMI data are due at 0815 GMT, Germany's at 0830 GMT and the eurozone's at 0900 GMT. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: Ant Group's Profit Soars on Investment Gains
data.description:
Ant Group's profit surged more than 50 times, driven by investment gains, contributing 4.49 billion yuan to Alibaba , which logged an estimated 13.6 billion profit for the quarter ended Sept. 30, 2024, Bloomberg News reported Thursday.

The surge stemmed from a net investment gain, reversing losses from the previous year, as well as strong revenue growth, signaling a rebound in its commerce business and AI expansion, according to the report.

China's tech sector is seeing renewed optimism after President Xi Jinping met with top entrepreneurs, including Ant Group founder Jack Ma, Bloomberg wrote.

Meanwhile, Ant is advancing AI efforts, developing a humanoid robotics unit and launching digital services within its Alipay app. The company also restructured key units to prepare for potential spinoffs.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, though rebounding inflation and low consumer confidence suggest the outlook for the sector remains uncertain.

U.K. retail sales volumes were 1.7% higher in January than in the prior month, from a fall of 0.6% in December, the Office for National Statistics said Friday. Economists expected sales to climb 0.9% on month, according to a poll by The Wall Street Journal.

Food store sales volumes jumped 5.6%, the largest rise since March 2020, following falls in recent months, the ONS said.

However, retailers indicated that the increase was because of more people eating at home in January. That suggests retailers' gains come at a loss for restauranteurs and the boost to the overall economy will be smaller than these figures suggest, according to Capital Economics economist Paul Dales.

While consumer confidence in the U.K. edged up this month in data also published Friday, likely lifted by the Bank of England's interest-rate cut earlier in February, it remains at a low level. Indeed, sales fell over the three months to January and remain below prepandemic levels, the ONS said.

Elsewhere, inflation jumped to 3.0% in January, from 2.5% in the prior month, well above the central bank's target.

"The recent flatlining in employment and rebound in inflation may be offsetting the effects of the cuts in interest rates and holding back households' willingness to spend," Dales added.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Philippines Cuts Reserve Ratio to 5%
data.description:
The central bank of the Philippines reduced the reserve requirement ratio for universal and commercial banks by 200 basis points to 5%, marking the first RRR cut of the year.

Additionally, the BSP lowered the RRR for digital banks to 2.5% and for thrift banks to 0%.

These adjustments will take effect on March 28 and apply to local currency deposits and deposit substitute liabilities of both banks and non-bank financial institutions with quasi-banking functions.

The central bank highlighted that reducing the reserve requirement ratios will help alleviate barriers that impede effective financial intermediation.

Moreover, the central bank reaffirmed its long-term objective of empowering banks to allocate their funds more efficiently toward productive loans and investments.

data.title: Nissan stock surges on report it's seeking Tesla deal. How the odd pairing could work.
data.description:
By Steve Goldstein

Nissan Motor stock shot higher on Friday after a published report said the Japanese automaker was pursuing a deal to be bought by a Tesla-led consortium.

Nissan shares (JP:7201) rose as much as 13% in Tokyo - settling 9% higher - as the Financial Times, citing three people with "direct knowledge," said a high-level Japanese group is drawing up plans for Elon Musk's Tesla (TSLA) to invest in the automaker. The group leading the plan includes a former Japanese prime minister, Yoshihide Suga, as well as Hiro Mizuno, a former Tesla board member, the FT reported.

Nissan recently saw merger talks with Honda (JP:7267) collapse.

For Tesla, the report says, the benefit of a Nissan deal would be the ability to buy its U.S. plants.

Nissan has two plants in Tennessee and one in Mississippi, employing over 12,000 people. Tesla makes all of its vehicles sold in the U.S. locally but does source components from Mexico and other countries.

Nissan's U.S. plants only produced 525,000 units last year, while they had capacity to produce 1 million units, the FT report said.

A Tesla investment would help fend off Nissan interest from Foxconn, though the report said the iPhone assembler could still play a part as a minority investor.

-Steve Goldstein

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Teck Resources Ltd reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Teck Resources Ltd reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 60 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of C$1.40. The mean expectation of nineteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 35 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 12 cents to 63 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 22.6% to C$2.86 billion from a year ago; analysts expected C$2.78 billion.

  • Teck Resources Ltd's reported EPS for the quarter was a loss of C$1.45​.

  • The company reported a quarterly loss of C$748 million.

  • Teck Resources Ltd shares had risen by 5.5% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 14.5% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 16 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the diversified mining peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Teck Resources Ltd is C$73.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 21 at 08:05 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in Canadian dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
Dow, S&P 500 Move Lower as Walmart Stock Falls

Major stock indexes fell, with investors unsettled by a note of caution in Walmart's forecast. Big bank stocks fell sharply despite no clear news catalyst, which some analysts attributed to economic concerns..

BOJ's Ueda Vows to Respond to Abnormal Bond Yield Rise

Bank of Japan Gov. Kazuo Ueda promised to respond to abnormal rises in bond yields, as expectations for monetary tightening sent Japanese government bond yields to multiyear highs.

Uptick in Japan Inflation Boosts Rate Hike Hopes

Japan's consumer inflation increased in January because of higher energy and food costs, likely adding fuel to speculation that an interest-rate hike is coming soon.

U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, largely from a recovery in the food segment.

Musk Looks at Auditing the Federal Reserve

The billionaire gives remarks at the Conservative Political Action Conference.

Australia Central Bank Says it Won't Commit to Any Set Course on Rates

The Reserve Bank of Australia isn't committed to any course of action on interest rates with Gov. Michele Bullock cautioning that easing policy too quickly may stoke a fresh wave of inflationary pressures.

Funding Cuts at CFPB Seen Leading to 'Regulatory Vacuum' for Big Banks

Turmoil at the agency could harm not only consumers but also small state-chartered banks, as their bigger rivals gain advantage from lax CFPB supervision, 23 state attorneys general say in court filing.

Tech stocks seem to have ended their 'difficult relationship' with the bond market

At least for now, U.S. large-cap technology and growth stocks appear "free of their difficult relationship with long-term yields," according to DataTrek Research.

Buffett's Letter Is Due Saturday; Succession, Dividend, Stock Market Could Be Addressed

This is Buffett's 60th year at Berkshire's helm and the company has record operating earnings in the first nine months of 2024 and a record stock price.

U.S. Aluminum Buyers Scramble for Metal as Trump Tariff Looms

Canada supplies roughly 60% to 70% of the imports that come into the U.S. market. Canadian suppliers like Rio Tinto produce high quality aluminum that is typically sold to stateside buyers.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 AM ET
data.description:
Moody's Cuts Nissan Motor's Rating to Junk Status

Moody's said the rating action reflects the Japanese automaker's weak profitability driven by slowing demand for its aging model portfolio.

CATL, Volkswagen to Collaborate on EV Batteries Amid Intensifying Competition

The Chinese battery giant will collaborate with Volkswagen on electric vehicle batteries, new materials and other auto parts.

Air Liquide Net Profit Misses Views

The industrial-gases company said net profit for 2024 rose to EUR3.31 billion but missed analysts' expectations on a decline in sales.

Standard Chartered Net Profit Falls, Announces $1.5 Billion Buyback
